message:
fix(google-docs,google-sheets): block double-percent-encoded path segments (br-rjok0)

P3 — connectors/google-{docs,sheets}/src/client.rs::sanitize_path_segment
already rejects single-percent-encoded traversal markers (%2f / %5c /
%3f / %23). It missed the double-encoded variants: a server that decodes
the request path twice (some proxies and sidecars do) would unwrap
`%252F` → `%2F` → `/`, resurrecting the traversal that the lowercase
checks were meant to block.

Fix: also refuse any segment whose lowercased form contains `%25` (a
literal `%` percent-encoded). Drive document_ids and Sheets
spreadsheet_ids never legitimately contain a `%`, so this is a
defense-in-depth no-op for valid input.

Regression tests pin: %252F (and lowercase variant), %2523 (and
uppercase fragment-suffix variant), and bare %25.

Verified via cargo test -p fcp-google-docs / -p fcp-google-sheets
sanitize_path_segment — 3/3 + 3/3 pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
